text:
There was once a village far away where nothing ever grew because everyone was afraid of heights. That's how the village stayed stagnant.

One summer afternoon, a young girl named Daisy dared to climb up the tallest oak tree. Her friends hadn't dared to go higher than old Mrs. Owlfrobe. When she finally reached the moonflowers at its peak—

Suddenly—

A thunderstorm erupted.

Raindrops pelted her hair. Birds roared overhead.

And there she stayed, sheltered under an owl's wing. When the storm passed, she climbed down feeling grateful.

Later, she told her friends how she'd embraced the night.

They laughed.

But every dawn after, Daisy was eager to climb again. One thing changed; she brought chocolate. Birds liked sweets too.

The village grew smaller.

But Daisy's oak tree flourished.

From its branches hung night butterflies. And sometimes, at twilight—

They'd see Daisy listening.

That's the end of that story.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
